decisions.decision: Li criterion investigation complete — fundamental circularity identified
decisions.rationale: Proved Gamma > 0 for n >= 8 (analytical, exact formula). But bounding |zeta part| IS equivalent to RH: singularity structure of generating function F(x) = log_sz(1/(1-x)) directly encodes zero locations. Off-critical-line zero -> singularity inside unit disk -> exponential growth -> lambda_n < 0. No unconditional shortcut.

decisions.decision: Key analytical results worth documenting as preprint
decisions.rationale: The smooth_gamma + log_sz decomposition, exact g_k formula, N_0=11 threshold, and oscillation period = 2pi/arg(1-1/rho_1) are publishable observations even without proving RH.

decisions.decision: Connes prolate operator (Avenue 5) deferred to dedicated session
decisions.rationale: Requires web research into 2024-2025 papers. Entirely different framework — not a continuation of Li criterion work.

next_action: Choose direction: (1) Write Li criterion preprint documenting S17-19 findings, (2) Avenue 5: Connes prolate operator research session, (3) Return to M-function with sharpened tools, (4) Jensen polynomial connection (from S16 dbn_jensen.py). The Li investigation reached its natural conclusion — the decomposition is clean but the core difficulty (bounding the zeta part) is equivalent to RH itself.
context_notes: Sessions 17-19 (2026-03-28/29) explored Li's criterion thoroughly. 8 scripts: li_criterion.py, li_prime_anatomy.py, li_extended.py, li_highorder.py, li_M_connection.py, li_asymptotics.py, li_euler_product.py, li_bombieri_lagarias.py, li_gamma_proof.py, li_zeta_bound.py. The decomposition lambda_n = lambda_n^Gamma + lambda_n^zeta is the central framework. Gamma part has exact formula g_k = (-1)^k*[(1-2^{-k})zeta(k)-1]/k. Python: /c/Python313/python.exe.
